behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the heuristic’s route-quality logic itself (algorithmic correctness), which can be exercised with in-memory coordinate sets and an exact optimal solver for comparison. No real network/OS/subprocess and no AI output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run deterministic in-memory stop sets (<=20), compute heuristic route length vs exact optimal length, and assert ratio <= 1.5 for each case.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic math/config application at function/object boundary: distance segment + road-type speed -> time window. The behavior text does not require real infrastructure; mocks do not hide the core failure.
behaviors.test_description: Given fixed segment distances and road-type speed config, assert estimated per-leg and total time windows match expected values, including rounding/boundary cases.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: Pure validation/arithmetic rule (compare totals and compute excess). Real I/O is unnecessary; failure is local logic.
behaviors.test_description: Pass estimated route durations and remaining shift-hour values to validator; assert reject/accept decision and exact excess value when over limit.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module handoff: geocoder result handling -> filtering/skipped reporting -> optimizer input. A stub geocoder returning no-results is sufficient; real HTTP is not required by this behavior text. Rejected System because behavior explicitly targets logical handling of a no-result response, not transport/network failure.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service with in-process geocoder stub (one address returns empty result), optimizer, and reporter; assert skipped-address list includes failed address and final route excludes it while optimizing remaining stops.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: This behavior spans deduplication/state-merge plus downstream routing consumption (“not visited twice”), so inter-module wiring is the realistic failure boundary. No real OS/network/subprocess and no AI output dependency. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is deterministic data orchestration only.
behaviors.test_description: Run service path with duplicate addresses carrying distinct item payloads; assert one consolidated stop with merged items is produced and final route contains that stop exactly once.
